name: Bulgur Breads
recipeIngredient: Approximately 2 cups unbleached hard white or all-purpose flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the bulgur and salt in a food processor. Pour the boiling water over, and stir or process briefly to mix. Let stand for thirty minutes.
2. When you return to the bulgur mixture, it will appear as if the bulgur has totally absorbed the water. Add 1 cup flour and the minced onion, and process for 1 minute. The dough should come together into a large ball as you process. If it doesn't, feel the dough: If it feels dry and floury, start the processor, add 2 tablespoons warm water, and process for 30 seconds; if the bulgur and flour still don't come together, add another 2 tablespoons water and process.
3. Once a ball of dough forms, continue processing for 2 to 3 minutes longer. Turn out onto a well-floured surface. (If your processor is too small to handle the whole dough, work with half at a time, adding 1/2 cup flour to each portion, then combine when you turn it out and knead by hand).
4. Knead by hand for 4 to 5 minutes.
5. Alternatively, if you do not have a food processor, combine bulgur, salt, and boiling water in a medium-sized bowl and let stand for 30 minutes. Add onions and 1/2 cup flour, then stir in. Add another 1/2 cup flour and use your hands to fold the flour into the dough. With a little persistence, you will soon have a dough (although somewhat sticky) that you can knead.
6. Turn out onto a well-floured kneading surface and knead for 6 or 7 minutes. The dough will be sticky at first but will soon come together into a smooth, workable dough; you may be surprised to discover how kneadable bulgur is.
7. When finished kneading, cover the dough with plastic wrap and let stand until ready to proceed further, for 15 minutes to an hour, depending on your schedule.
8. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit with baking stones or quarry tiles in place on a rack in the bottom third of the oven.
9. Divide the dough into 8 pieces and flatten each on a well-floured bread board. Using a rolling pin, roll out one or two breads until very thin, about 8 to 10 inches in diameter. Work with only those that will fit in your oven at once.
10. When ready, handle the bread gently as you place it on the hot tiles. Dusting a peel or the back of a baking sheet with flour and using a peel or sheet to transfer the breads to hot tiles is also an option.
11. Bake on one side for 1 1/2 to 2 minutes, then turn over to bake the other side for another 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. The bread should begin to brown around the outside when baked.
12. Keep the breads warm by stacking them and wrapping them in a clean kitchen towel.
Yield: 8 thin, supple, slightly chewy flatbreads between 8 and 10 inches in diameter.
